children.title: General office clerks
children.rationale: AI can already handle most of what general office clerks do — sorting and filing documents, drafting routine correspondence, processing data, and routing inquiries. A score of 7 reflects that the core work is almost entirely information-processing, with only modest barriers from physical mail handling and organisational context.

children.title: Secretaries (general)
children.rationale: AI can already draft emails, transcribe documents, manage calendars, sort mail, and handle routine correspondence with minimal human input. The job's core output is digital information processing with little physical or high-stakes interpersonal content, leaving only coordination and oversight roles for humans.

children.title: Data entry clerks
children.rationale: AI and automation tools can handle virtually all data entry work — receiving documents, typing in numbers, verifying accuracy, and moving data between systems — with greater speed and fewer errors than humans. This is the textbook case of full automation, matching the maximum-exposure anchor.

children.title: Waiters
children.title_de: Chefs de service, Servicefach- und Servicehilfskräfte in Restaurants
children.rationale: Waiting tables is almost entirely physical and interpersonal — carrying food, pouring drinks, reading the room, and handling complaints face-to-face. AI can handle reservations and payments digitally, but the core job requires a human body in a dining room.

children.title: Building caretakers
children.rationale: AI cannot mop floors, fix leaky pipes, patrol hallways, or tend boilers — the core of this job is physical presence and hands-on maintenance. A small share of administrative tasks like filling forms or writing reports can be AI-assisted, but these are minor compared to the physical work.

children.title: Cooks
children.rationale: Cooking is fundamentally a physical craft — chopping, grilling, plating, and tasting require hands, heat, and real-time sensory judgment that robots cannot yet reliably replicate in unstructured kitchen environments. AI can help with menu planning or recipe lookup, but the core work stays human.

children.title: Cleaning and housekeeping supervisors in offices, hotels and other establishments
children.title_de: Reinigungs- und Hauswirtschaftleiter und -kräfte in Büros, Hotels und anderen Einrichtungen
children.rationale: AI cannot sweep floors, make beds, clean bathrooms, or physically inspect rooms — the core work is hands-on and requires constant movement through physical spaces. Some scheduling and reporting tasks can be assisted by AI, but these are minor compared to the physical and interpersonal demands.

children.title: Hairdressers
children.rationale: AI cannot cut, colour, or style hair — the entire job requires skilled hands working directly on a person's head. A few peripheral tasks like booking appointments can be automated, but the core physical craft keeps this occupation firmly low-exposure.

children.title: Beauticians and related workers
children.rationale: AI cannot apply creams, give massages, shape nails, or perform waxing — the entire core of this job requires skilled physical touch on a client's body. The work is almost entirely hands-on and interpersonal, keeping AI exposure very low.

children.title: Travel attendants and travel stewards
children.rationale: AI cannot greet passengers, serve meals, demonstrate safety procedures, administer first aid, or physically evacuate an aircraft — the core of this job is in-person physical and interpersonal service. The role is anchored by safety-critical presence and human touch, keeping AI exposure very low.

children.title: Bartenders
children.rationale: Bartending is almost entirely physical and interpersonal — mixing drinks, handling glassware, managing intoxicated customers, and creating atmosphere cannot be done by AI. Payment processing is the only meaningfully automatable task, keeping this score very low.

children.title: Pet groomers and animal care workers
children.rationale: AI cannot bathe, groom, feed, restrain, or physically care for animals — the core of this job is hands-on work in unpredictable physical environments. Some record-keeping and scheduling tasks are automatable, but these are minor relative to the physical demands.

children.title: Driving instructors
children.rationale: AI can handle classroom theory, traffic law quizzes, and progress tracking, but the core job is sitting in a car with a nervous beginner and physically intervening to prevent accidents. That irreducible physical presence and real-time safety responsibility keeps this occupation firmly low-exposure.

children.title: Transport conductors
children.rationale: AI can automate ticket checking and passenger information via apps and automated gates, but the core job involves physical presence on vehicles, assisting passengers, managing emergencies, and ensuring safety in real time. The irreducibly physical and interpersonal nature of the role keeps AI exposure low.

children.title: Travel guides
children.rationale: AI can generate travel information, answer tourist questions, and plan itineraries, but guiding requires physical presence, real-time crowd management, and interpersonal performance that AI cannot replicate in 2026. The job is fundamentally about being there in person, keeping the score low.

children.title: Undertakers and embalmers
children.rationale: AI cannot embalm bodies, prepare graves, dress remains, or provide the human presence grieving families need during funeral arrangements. The work is almost entirely physical and interpersonal, leaving very little for AI to replace.

children.title: Personal services workers not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Berufe im Bereich personenbezogener Dienstleistungen, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI cannot accompany someone to a nightclub, act as a dancing partner, or provide physical companionship — the core value is embodied human presence. The job is almost entirely physical and interpersonal, leaving very little for AI to automate.

children.title: Domestic housekeepers
children.rationale: AI cannot vacuum floors, make beds, cook meals, or clean bathrooms — the core of this job is physical work in someone's home. A few tasks like budgeting or ordering supplies could be AI-assisted, but these are minor compared to the hands-on physical work that defines the role.

children.title: Companions and valets
children.rationale: AI cannot iron clothes, make beds, clean rooms, cook meals, or physically accompany someone to activities — the entire job is hands-on personal service. Almost nothing here is digital, so AI offers virtually no substitution.

children.title: Astrologers, fortune-tellers and related workers
children.rationale: AI can already generate horoscopes, interpret birth charts, and produce personalised fortune-telling content at scale. However, the core value clients pay for is the live interpersonal ritual, emotional connection, and perceived psychic presence — which AI cannot replicate.

title: Business and Administration Associate Professionals
title_de: Betriebswirtschaftliche und kaufmännische Fachkräfte und Verwaltungsfachkräfte

children.title: Accounting associate professionals
children.rationale: AI can already automate bookkeeping entries, reconcile accounts, prepare financial statements, and flag errors with high accuracy. The work is almost entirely digital information processing with limited physical or interpersonal demands, placing this occupation firmly in the very-high-exposure tier.

children.title: Administrative and executive secretaries
children.rationale: AI can already draft emails, schedule meetings, transcribe audio, manage documents, and prepare reports — the core daily tasks of this role. Enough high-value coordination, stakeholder liaison, and judgment work remains to keep humans in the loop, but the role is significantly reshaped.

children.title: Commercial sales representatives
children.rationale: AI can automate quoting prices, drafting proposals, analysing sales data, and managing follow-up emails, but closing deals still depends on reading people and building trust face-to-face. The job sits in the high tier because routine information tasks are heavily automatable, yet the relational core resists full substitution.

children.title: Real estate agents and property managers
children.rationale: AI can automate property valuation, market research, contract drafting, and financial analysis — tasks that make up a large share of this job. However, client negotiation, physical property inspections, and trust-based relationship management keep human agents essential.

children.title: Insurance representatives
children.rationale: AI can already automate much of the data gathering, risk calculation, and product comparison that insurance agents do daily. However, building client trust, negotiating complex contracts, and advising on sensitive personal situations still require a human.

children.title: Office supervisors
children.rationale: AI can automate data analysis, report writing, scheduling, and call quality monitoring — tasks that make up a large share of this role. However, managing staff conflicts, coaching employees, and coordinating across departments still require human judgment and presence.

children.title: Medical secretaries
children.rationale: AI can already handle medical transcription, appointment scheduling, insurance form completion, and records management — the core outputs of this role. Human value remains in patient-facing interactions, complex coordination, and accountability within regulated healthcare environments.

children.title: Buyers
children.rationale: AI can automate supplier research, bid comparison, price tracking, and purchase order generation, but core value lies in negotiating contracts and managing supplier relationships. The mix of digital information work and interpersonal negotiation keeps exposure at moderate levels.

children.title: Trade brokers
children.rationale: AI can automate market analysis, document preparation, and freight rate comparisons, but the core of this job — negotiating deals and building trust with counterparties — still requires human judgment and relationships. The mix of automatable back-office tasks and irreplaceable relationship-driven deal-making lands this in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Securities and finance dealers and brokers
children.rationale: AI can already analyse market trends, screen financial instruments, generate investment reports, and execute routine trades faster and more accurately than humans. The remaining human value lies in client relationships, regulatory accountability, and judgment in novel market conditions — keeping this just below maximum exposure.

children.title: Government social benefits officials
children.rationale: AI can automate eligibility checks, document review, and routine application processing, but fraud investigation, client interviews, and discretionary benefit decisions still require human judgment. The mix of structured information work and interpersonal/legal complexity places this occupation at moderate exposure.

children.title: Conference and event planners
children.rationale: AI can draft event proposals, manage registrations, and handle vendor research, but the core work—negotiating contracts, coordinating on-site logistics, and managing client relationships in real time—requires human presence and judgment. The job is a mix of automatable planning tasks and irreplaceable physical coordination.

children.title: Clearing and forwarding agents
children.rationale: AI can automate customs document classification, tariff coding, and compliance checks — the core paperwork-heavy tasks of this role. However, negotiating with carriers, resolving shipment problems, and managing regulatory exceptions still require human judgment, keeping exposure in the high-but-not-very-high range.

children.title: Police inspectors and detectives
children.rationale: AI can help analyse documents, search databases, and draft reports, but core detective work—interviewing suspects, preserving crime scenes, making arrests—requires physical presence and human judgment. The job stays at moderate exposure because most high-value tasks cannot be done remotely or algorithmically.

children.title: Customs and border inspectors
children.rationale: AI can assist with document verification, database checks, and flagging anomalies in cargo manifests, but the core job requires physical presence at borders, real-time human judgment, and authority to detain people. The irreducibly physical and legal-authority nature of the work keeps AI exposure low.

children.title: Valuers and loss assessors
children.rationale: AI can already automate much of the document analysis, claims classification, and report drafting that underpins this work. However, physical site inspections, fraud interviews, and judgment calls on ambiguous damage or value require human presence and expertise that AI cannot yet replace.

children.title: Government tax and excise officials
children.rationale: AI can already inspect tax returns, flag discrepancies, calculate liabilities, and cross-reference financial records with high accuracy. Human judgment remains essential for contested cases, fraud investigations, and legally binding enforcement decisions.

children.title: Legal secretaries
children.rationale: AI can draft and review legal documents, manage schedules, and handle routine correspondence with high accuracy. However, physical document handling, court liaison, civil registration duties, and confidentiality-sensitive client interactions keep meaningful human involvement necessary.

children.title: Employment agents and contractors
children.rationale: AI can already screen CVs, match candidates to job postings, and search talent databases faster than humans. However, the core value of this job—building trust with both employers and job seekers through personal relationships and judgment—still requires a human.

children.title: Government licensing officials
children.title_de: Fachkräfte bei staatlichen Pass-, Lizenz- und Genehmigungsstellen
children.rationale: AI can automate document checking, application routing, and correspondence drafting, but final licensing decisions require legal judgment, fraud detection, and accountability under public law. Human oversight remains essential for exceptions, discretionary rulings, and public trust in government authority.

children.title: Credit and loans officers
children.title_de: Fachkräfte im Bereich Kredit und Darlehen
children.rationale: AI can already automate credit scoring, analyse financial statements, assess loan risk, and flag fraud with high accuracy. Human judgment remains essential for complex cases, client interviews, and accountability for final credit decisions.

children.title: Regulatory government associate professionals not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Fachkräfte in der öffentlichen Verwaltung, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI can help draft inspection reports, analyse compliance documents, and flag anomalies in data, but the core work involves physical site visits, on-the-spot judgment, and legal enforcement authority. The job's irreducible field component keeps AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Business services agents not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Fachkräfte für unternehmensbezogene Dienstleistungen, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI can handle market research, drafting contracts, and preparing sales reports, but the core work—negotiating deals, building trust with artists or clients, and reading rooms at auctions—depends on human judgment and relationships. The mix of high-touch interpersonal roles and routine administrative tasks lands this occupation in the moderate tier.

children.title: Statistical, mathematical and related associate professionals
children.rationale: AI can now perform the core tasks of this role — running statistical calculations, identifying data patterns, preparing charts and reports — at professional quality. The job is almost entirely information-processing work with few physical or interpersonal anchors, leaving little that AI cannot assist or replace.

children.title: Primary school teachers
children.rationale: AI can help prepare lesson plans, grade homework, and generate teaching materials, but the core job is physically present with children — managing behaviour, supervising safety, and building relationships. The irreducibly human, in-person nature of primary teaching keeps AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Secondary education teachers
children.rationale: AI can draft lesson plans, generate practice tests, and give personalised feedback on student work, but classroom management, real-time adaptation to student needs, and the trusted adult relationship at the heart of teaching resist automation. The score reflects meaningful AI assistance without near-term substitution.

children.title: Early childhood educators
children.rationale: AI cannot supervise toddlers, resolve physical conflicts, guide toilet habits, or provide the embodied emotional presence that defines this work. The job is almost entirely physical caregiving and in-person relationship-building with very young children, keeping AI exposure very low.

children.title: University and higher education teachers
children.rationale: AI can draft lecture content, grade routine assignments, summarize research literature, and generate course materials, but live teaching, mentoring students, and producing original research remain human-led. The mix of automatable administrative tasks and irreplaceable interpersonal and creative work places this occupation in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Special needs teachers
children.rationale: AI can help prepare lesson plans, compile materials, and track student progress records, but the core work — building trust with vulnerable children, reading subtle emotional and physical cues, and adapting moment-to-moment in person — resists automation. The job's irreducibly physical and relational nature keeps exposure moderate.

children.title: Vocational education teachers
children.rationale: AI can help prepare lesson materials, grade written work, and track student progress, but the core job involves hands-on demonstration, real-time coaching, and safety supervision in workshops and labs. The irreplaceable physical and interpersonal demands keep this at moderate exposure.

children.title: Other music teachers
children.rationale: AI can generate practice exercises, explain music theory, and provide feedback on recordings, but teaching an instrument requires physical demonstration, real-time correction of posture and technique, and personal mentorship. The core value is embodied, in-person instruction that AI cannot replicate in 2026.

children.title: Other language teachers
children.rationale: AI can now generate lesson materials, grade written work, and provide conversational practice to language learners with reasonable quality. However, the core value of this job lies in real-time human interaction, motivational coaching, and adapting to individual learners' emotional and cultural needs in ways AI still handles poorly.

children.title: Education methods specialists
children.rationale: AI can draft curriculum documents, analyse education data, and generate research summaries, but the core work—observing classrooms, coaching teachers, and advising institutions—requires human judgment and trust. The mix of information work and irreplaceable in-person evaluation keeps this at moderate exposure.

children.title: Teaching professionals not elsewhere classified
children.rationale: AI can automate content creation, lesson planning, and basic student progress tracking, but tutoring, counselling, and mentoring depend on human relationship and adaptive judgment. The group's wide heterogeneity—from e-learning architects to prison instructors—pulls the score toward the middle of the high tier.

children.title: Other arts teachers
children.rationale: AI can help plan lesson content or give feedback on student artwork, but the core job is physically demonstrating dance moves, directing live performances, and coaching students in person. The hands-on, embodied, and relational nature of arts teaching keeps AI substitution low.

children.title: Information technology trainers
children.rationale: AI can generate training materials, build e-learning modules, and draft curricula faster than any human trainer. However, live instruction, adapting to learner confusion in real time, and building credibility with adult IT professionals still require a human presence.

children.title: Nursing professionals
children.rationale: AI can assist with documentation, care planning, and screening decisions, but the core of nursing—physically assessing patients, administering treatments, and providing hands-on care—cannot be automated. The job is anchored in physical presence and interpersonal trust, keeping exposure moderate.

children.title: Specialist medical practitioners
children.rationale: AI can read medical scans, flag abnormal lab results, and draft clinical notes, but diagnosing complex cases, performing surgery, and managing patient relationships still require a human specialist. The irreplaceable physical, procedural, and high-stakes judgment demands keep exposure moderate.

children.title: Physiotherapists
children.rationale: AI can assist with documentation, treatment planning support, and analyzing patient data, but the core work—hands-on manual therapy, physical assessment, and therapeutic exercise—requires physical presence and skilled touch. The job is anchored in physical intervention, keeping AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Health professionals not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Spezialisten in Gesundheitsberufen, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI can assist with documentation, image analysis, and treatment planning support, but the core work—hands-on manipulation, physical assessment, and therapeutic touch—requires physical presence and cannot be automated. The job is anchored in embodied, interpersonal clinical care that AI cannot replicate.

children.title: Traditional and complementary medicine professionals
children.title_de: Spezialisten der traditionellen und komplementären Medizin
children.rationale: AI can support documentation, treatment planning research, and lifestyle advice, but the core work—physical examination, hands-on therapies like acupuncture or massage, and building therapeutic trust—requires in-person human presence. The heavily interpersonal and physical nature of the work keeps AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Pharmacists
children.rationale: AI can check drug interactions, flag prescription errors, and retrieve medicines information faster than humans, but dispensing physical medications, counselling patients face-to-face, and managing safety in complex clinical cases still require a licensed professional present. Regulatory accountability and physical dispensing duties keep this at moderate exposure.

children.title: Dentists
children.rationale: AI can assist with reading X-rays, flagging decay, and generating treatment plans, but drilling, extracting teeth, placing implants, and administering anaesthesia all require skilled hands-on work. The job is anchored in irreplaceable physical procedures and patient trust, keeping exposure moderate.

children.title: Audiologists and speech therapists
children.rationale: AI can assist with hearing test analysis, documentation, and screening, but the core work—hands-on patient assessment, therapeutic relationship-building, and treating swallowing or speech disorders—requires direct physical and interpersonal presence. The job stays mostly human-delivered despite useful AI tools at the margins.

children.title: Midwifery professionals
children.rationale: AI cannot deliver babies, perform physical examinations, or manage labour complications — the core of this job is hands-on clinical care in unpredictable, high-stakes physical situations. The small administrative and educational tasks AI could assist with are peripheral, keeping overall exposure low.

children.title: Veterinarians
children.rationale: AI can assist with diagnostic image analysis, literature review, and record-keeping, but the core work — physical examination, surgery, and hands-on treatment of animals — cannot be automated. The job remains anchored by irreplaceable physical and sensory tasks.

children.title: Generalist medical practitioners
children.rationale: AI can help draft clinical notes, flag diagnostic patterns, and suggest treatment options, but examining patients, building trust, and making judgment calls in ambiguous cases still require a doctor in the room. The physical, relational, and legal core of general practice keeps AI in a supporting role.

children.title: Dieticians and nutritionists
children.rationale: AI can analyze dietary data, generate meal plans, and provide nutrition education content, but clinical assessment of patients and therapeutic counseling require in-person judgment. The mix of information work and direct patient care keeps exposure at moderate levels.

children.title: Optometrists and ophthalmic opticians
children.rationale: AI can assist with interpreting diagnostic scans and flagging retinal abnormalities, but the core work—physically examining eyes, fitting lenses, and managing treatment—requires hands-on clinical skill. The job stays at moderate exposure because most value is delivered through in-person examination and patient interaction, not information processing alone.

children.title: Environmental and occupational health and hygiene professionals
children.title_de: Spezialisten in den Bereichen Umwelt- und Arbeitsmedizin sowie Hygiene
children.rationale: AI can help draft risk assessments, analyse monitoring data, and check regulatory compliance documents, but the core work requires physical site inspections, hands-on hazard measurement, and regulatory accountability that AI cannot replace. The job stays at moderate exposure because most value is delivered in the field, not at a desk.

children.title: Shop sales assistants
children.rationale: AI can handle inventory tracking, payment processing, and product information queries, but the core job involves physical presence — stocking shelves, demonstrating products, and reading customers face-to-face. The irreducible physical and interpersonal demands keep exposure moderate.

children.title: Shop supervisors
children.rationale: AI can help with scheduling, inventory tracking, and sales reporting, but the core job involves supervising people on a shop floor, handling staff conflicts, and responding to real-time customer situations. The heavy reliance on physical presence and interpersonal judgment keeps AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Cashiers and ticket clerks
children.rationale: AI and self-checkout technology can already handle scanning, payment processing, and receipt issuance with minimal human involvement. The remaining human value lies in customer service, handling exceptions, and physical tasks like bagging — keeping the score just below the highest tier.

children.title: Shop keepers
children.rationale: AI can automate inventory tracking, financial record-keeping, and pricing analysis, but the core of this job — buying goods, advising customers face-to-face, and managing a physical shop — resists automation. The mix of physical presence, supplier relationships, and hands-on retail operations keeps exposure moderate.

children.title: Stall and market salespersons
children.rationale: AI cannot set up a market stall, handle physical goods, or engage customers face-to-face in an outdoor market setting. The job is fundamentally physical and interpersonal, with only minor administrative tasks like record-keeping that AI could assist with.

children.title: Service station attendants
children.rationale: AI can automate payment processing and stock reporting, but the core job involves physically fuelling vehicles, checking tyre pressure, washing windows, and doing minor repairs on a forecourt. The hands-on, outdoor, customer-facing nature of most tasks keeps AI exposure low.

children.title: Sales demonstrators
children.rationale: AI cannot physically set up displays, hand out samples, or demonstrate products in person to shoppers. The job's core value is live, embodied persuasion and human rapport at the point of sale, which keeps AI exposure low.

children.title: Sales workers not elsewhere classified
children.rationale: AI can automate booking reservations, process payments, generate rental contracts, and handle routine customer queries. However, physical vehicle inspection, damage assessment, and in-person customer service keep full automation limited, landing this role in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Food service counter attendants
children.rationale: AI cannot hand food to customers, clean equipment, or physically prepare and portion meals at a counter. The job is almost entirely hands-on physical and interpersonal work, leaving very little for AI to automate.

children.title: Contact centre salespersons
children.rationale: AI can already make outbound sales calls, follow scripts, handle objections, update CRM records, and schedule appointments — the entire core workflow of this job. This is one of the most directly replaceable roles by conversational AI agents available today.

children.title: Fashion and other models
children.rationale: AI can generate photorealistic images of virtual models and clothing, reducing demand for some commercial photography work. However, live runway shows, physical fittings, art modeling, and brand authenticity requirements keep significant human demand intact.

children.title: Door to door salespersons
children.rationale: Door-to-door sales requires physically visiting homes, reading social cues in real time, and persuading skeptical strangers face-to-face — tasks AI cannot perform in 2026. The job's core value is human presence and interpersonal trust-building, keeping AI exposure low.

children.title: Civil engineering technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with report writing, energy audits, and data analysis, but the core work—inspecting physical sites, testing materials, and enforcing safety on construction sites—requires in-person presence and physical judgment. The job stays at moderate exposure because most high-value tasks happen in the field, not at a desk.

children.title: Draughtspersons
children.rationale: AI-powered CAD tools can now automate large portions of technical drawing, pattern grading, and circuit board layout tasks that form the core of this job. Human value remains in interpreting ambiguous engineering requirements, client consultation, and validating designs against physical constraints.

children.title: Construction supervisors
children.rationale: AI can help with scheduling, progress tracking, and documentation, but the core job is physically supervising workers and inspecting construction sites in person. The hands-on, safety-critical, and unpredictable nature of construction sites keeps AI substitution low.

children.title: Manufacturing supervisors
children.title_de: Produktionsleiter bei der Herstellung von Waren
children.rationale: AI can automate scheduling, reporting, and production planning, but the core job requires physical presence on the factory floor to supervise workers, ensure safety, and respond to real-time equipment or quality issues. The irreducible physical oversight and people-management demands keep exposure moderate.

children.title: Physical and engineering science technicians not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Material- und ingenieurtechnische Fachkräfte, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI can assist with data analysis, report writing, and CAD-based design tasks, but most of this work involves hands-on equipment testing, physical inspection, and on-site troubleshooting that AI cannot yet perform. The wide mix of physical and technical sub-roles keeps overall exposure moderate.

children.title: Mechanical engineering technicians
children.title_de: Maschinenbautechniker, Techniker im Bereich Systemtechnik, Automation und Unternehmensprozesse
children.rationale: AI can assist with engineering design, drawing interpretation, data analysis, and cost estimation, but core tasks like physical assembly, on-site troubleshooting, and hands-on testing resist automation. The mix of digital design work and irreplaceable physical presence keeps this at moderate exposure.

children.title: Chemical and physical science technicians
children.rationale: AI can automate data logging, pattern recognition in sensor readings, and routine report writing, but the core work—collecting physical samples, calibrating instruments, and running hands-on lab tests—requires physical presence. The job is anchored in laboratory and field environments that resist remote automation.

children.title: Electrical engineering technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with drafting wiring diagrams, reading blueprints, and preparing cost estimates, but the core work — physically installing, testing, calibrating, and repairing electrical systems — requires hands-on presence in unstructured environments. The job is anchored by physical manipulation and on-site judgment, keeping AI exposure low.

children.title: Aircraft pilots and related associate professionals
children.rationale: AI can assist with flight planning, weather analysis, and monitoring instruments, but actually flying an aircraft requires real-time physical control, split-second judgment, and regulatory certification that AI cannot replace in 2026. Safety-critical liability, certification requirements, and passenger trust keep human pilots firmly in the cockpit.

children.title: Life science technicians (excluding medical)
children.rationale: AI can help analyze experimental data, run simulations, and manage lab databases, but the core work—calibrating instruments, preparing biological samples, and running physical laboratory tests—requires hands-on presence. The job is anchored in physical lab work, keeping AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Incinerator and water treatment plant operators
children.rationale: AI can automate data logging, anomaly detection, and process optimization in plant control systems, but the core job requires physical presence to inspect equipment, collect samples, and respond to malfunctions in industrial environments. Regulatory accountability and hands-on intervention keep this firmly in the moderate tier.

children.title: Air traffic controllers
children.rationale: AI can assist with flight plan analysis, weather data processing, and conflict detection, but directing live aircraft in real time requires split-second human judgment under life-or-death conditions. Strict safety regulation, liability, and the irreversible consequences of error keep human controllers central for the foreseeable future.

children.title: Forestry technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with forest inventory data analysis and mapping, but the core work—planting trees, operating heavy equipment, fighting fires, and supervising physical harvesting operations—must happen outdoors in unpredictable terrain. The job is overwhelmingly physical and environmental, keeping AI exposure very low.

children.title: Electronics engineering technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with circuit design, reading schematics, and analysing test data, but the core work involves physically assembling, soldering, testing, and repairing electronic hardware. The hands-on, physical nature of most tasks keeps AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Power production plant operators
children.rationale: AI can automate monitoring dashboards and flag anomalies in sensor data, but operating turbines, responding to equipment failures, and maintaining physical plant machinery require hands-on intervention in complex industrial environments. The job is anchored in physical oversight and real-time safety response, keeping AI exposure low.

children.title: Ships' deck officers and pilots
children.rationale: AI can assist with route planning, weather analysis, and navigation calculations, but physically commanding a vessel, docking in congested ports, and managing emergencies at sea require real-time human judgment and presence. Heavy regulatory accountability, physical unpredictability, and safety-critical decision-making keep AI substitution very low.

children.title: Chemical engineering technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with data analysis, lab documentation, and process simulations, but the core work involves hands-on chemical plant monitoring, physical lab testing, and equipment calibration in industrial environments. The dominant physical and safety-critical nature of the role keeps AI exposure low.

children.title: Agricultural technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with data analysis, report writing, and pattern recognition in sensor or lab data, but the core work involves physical specimen collection, field inspections, and hands-on laboratory procedures. The job is anchored in physical environments and manual scientific work, keeping AI exposure low.

children.title: Process control technicians not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Techniker in der Prozesssteuerung, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI can optimize process parameters, flag anomalies, and assist with troubleshooting in automated manufacturing environments. However, the core work involves hands-on physical intervention—adjusting machinery, performing maintenance, and responding to real-world equipment failures—which keeps AI substitution limited.

children.title: Air traffic safety electronics technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with software debugging, documentation, and monitoring data analysis, but the core work—physically installing, calibrating, and repairing safety-critical navigation hardware—requires hands-on technical skill in regulated environments. Strict aviation safety certification requirements and the physical nature of the work keep AI exposure low.

children.title: Mining and metallurgical technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with data analysis, report writing, and monitoring sensor feeds, but the core work—collecting rock samples underground, inspecting mine safety conditions, and operating surveying equipment in hazardous physical environments—requires hands-on presence. Physical fieldwork, safety oversight, and unstructured underground environments keep AI substitution low.

children.title: Petroleum and natural gas refining plant operators
children.rationale: AI can assist with monitoring sensor data, writing production logs, and analysing process data, but the core job involves physical presence at a hazardous industrial plant, hands-on equipment adjustment, and emergency response. Safety regulations and the irreplaceable need for on-site human judgment keep AI exposure low.

children.title: Ships’ engineers
children.rationale: AI can assist with monitoring engine performance data, maintaining logs, and analyzing sensor readings, but the core work—physically inspecting, repairing, and operating ship machinery in harsh maritime environments—requires hands-on expertise AI cannot replicate. The job is fundamentally physical and safety-critical, keeping exposure low.

children.title: Advertising and marketing professionals
children.rationale: AI can now draft ad copy, run market research analysis, generate campaign briefs, and automate performance reporting at near-professional quality. Human judgment in brand strategy, client relationships, and creative direction keeps this below the highest tier.

children.title: Accountants
children.rationale: AI can already prepare tax returns, analyse financial statements, check accounting records, and draft financial reports at near-professional quality. The remaining human value lies in judgment on complex disputes, fraud investigations, and client advisory work — keeping this just below maximum exposure.

children.title: Management and organisation analysts
children.title_de: Spezialisten im Bereich Management- und Organisationsanalyse
children.rationale: AI can already draft business analyses, interpret financial statements, model processes, and generate strategic recommendations from structured data. However, the core value of this role—building client trust, navigating organizational politics, and translating ambiguous problems into actionable change—still requires human judgment.

children.title: Personnel and careers professionals
children.title_de: Berufsberater und -analytiker und vergleichbare Spezialisten der Personalwesen
children.rationale: AI can automate job posting, CV screening, interview scheduling, and draft job descriptions or reports, handling much of the administrative and analytical groundwork. However, the core value — counselling individuals, managing labour relations, and advising on sensitive personnel decisions — depends on human judgment and trust.

children.title: Financial and investment advisers
children.rationale: AI can already analyse financial data, model portfolios, forecast trends, and draft financial plans with high accuracy. However, the core value of this role—building client trust, interpreting ambiguous personal goals, and taking accountability for high-stakes financial decisions—keeps full automation at bay.

children.title: Technical and medical sales professionals (excluding ICT)
children.rationale: AI can automate lead generation, draft proposals, analyze market data, and handle routine customer inquiries for technical sales reps. However, the core value—building trust with clients, demonstrating complex products in person, and negotiating contracts—still requires human presence and judgment.

children.title: Financial analysts
children.rationale: AI can already automate the core work of financial analysts: processing financial statements, generating forecasts, screening securities, and drafting investment reports. Human value remains in client relationships, novel cross-market judgment, and accountability for recommendations.

children.title: Training and staff development professionals
children.rationale: AI can draft training materials, analyse skill gaps, and generate e-learning content, automating a significant share of content-creation and assessment tasks. However, live facilitation, coaching relationships, and organisational change management keep human trainers central.

children.title: Public relations professionals
children.rationale: AI can draft press releases, write content, analyse audience data, and manage social media scheduling at professional quality. However, the core value of PR — building trust, navigating political relationships, and managing crises — still depends heavily on human judgment and interpersonal credibility.

children.title: Policy administration professionals
children.title_de: Spezialisten in der strategischen Planung in Politik und Wirtschaft
children.rationale: AI can draft policy briefs, analyse legislation, research trends, and summarise stakeholder positions at near-professional quality. However, the job's core value lies in political judgment, stakeholder negotiation, and navigating institutional relationships — tasks AI cannot yet replace.

children.title: Information and communications technology sales professionals
children.title_de: Technische Verkaufsfachkräfte in der Informations- und Kommunikationstechnologie
children.rationale: AI can automate quote generation, sales reporting, CRM updates, and market research, but closing complex B2B ICT deals still requires human relationship-building and technical negotiation. The role sits at high exposure because most administrative and analytical tasks are automatable, but the core value—trusted advisory selling—resists substitution.

title: Building and Related Trades Workers
title_de: Bau- und Ausbaufachkräfte sowie verwandte Berufe, ausgenommen Elektriker

children.title: Carpenters and joiners
children.rationale: AI cannot cut timber, frame walls, fit doors, or install staircases — the core work is physical transformation of materials on a construction site. A few planning tasks like reading blueprints can be AI-assisted, but the job remains overwhelmingly hands-on.

children.title: Plumbers and pipe fitters
children.rationale: AI cannot bend pipes, dig trenches, solder joints, or install toilets and heating systems — the entire job is physical transformation of real-world infrastructure. A few planning tasks like reading blueprints may get AI assistance, but the hands-on work cannot be automated with 2026 technology.

children.title: Bricklayers and related workers
children.rationale: AI cannot lay bricks, mix mortar, or build walls — the entire job requires physical manipulation of heavy materials in unstructured outdoor environments. Robots capable of masonry work exist in early research but are far from replacing skilled tradespeople on Swiss construction sites.

children.title: Painters and related workers
children.rationale: AI cannot mix paint, prepare surfaces, hang wallpaper, or apply coatings to physical walls — the entire job involves manual transformation of real-world surfaces. A few peripheral tasks like quoting or record-keeping have AI tools, but the core physical craft is firmly human.

children.title: Floor layers and tile setters
children.rationale: AI cannot lay tiles, cut carpet, prepare surfaces, or install hardwood floors — all core tasks require skilled hands in unpredictable physical environments. The job is almost entirely manual craft work, leaving very little for AI to automate.

children.title: Plasterers
children.rationale: AI cannot mix plaster, cut drywall, or apply coatings to walls — the entire job involves physically transforming building surfaces using tools and materials. A few planning tasks like reading blueprints or ordering supplies have some AI assistance, but the core work is manual and keeps this score very low.

children.title: Insulation workers
children.rationale: Insulation work requires cutting materials to fit, physically installing them on pipes, walls, and equipment, and working in varied, unstructured construction environments — tasks that robots cannot reliably perform in 2026. The job is almost entirely hands-on physical work, leaving very little for AI to automate.

children.title: Spray painters and varnishers
children.rationale: AI cannot sand surfaces, mix and spray paint, or physically apply protective coatings to vehicles and manufactured parts. The work is almost entirely hands-on physical transformation of real objects, leaving very little for AI to automate.

children.title: Roofers
children.rationale: AI cannot cut and fit roofing materials, work at heights, or physically install tiles, flashing, and gutters on a real building. Almost every task requires hands-on physical work in unstructured outdoor environments, keeping AI exposure minimal.

children.title: Building frame and related trades workers not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Baukonstruktions- und verwandte Berufe, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI cannot climb chimneys, erect scaffolding, or demolish buildings — this work is entirely physical, performed at height or in hazardous environments requiring real-time human judgment and dexterity. Almost no core tasks are automatable with 2026 AI tools.

children.title: Concrete placers, concrete finishers and related workers
children.rationale: AI cannot pour concrete, screed surfaces, set terrazzo, or physically manipulate forms and reinforcement on a construction site. The work is almost entirely hands-on physical transformation of materials in unstructured outdoor environments, leaving very little for AI to automate.

children.title: Air conditioning and refrigeration mechanics
children.rationale: AI cannot weld pipes, braze refrigerant lines, or physically install compressors and ductwork — the core of this job is hands-on work in varied mechanical environments. The role stays low-exposure because nearly all outputs require physical presence and skilled manual manipulation.

children.title: Building structure cleaners
children.rationale: AI cannot physically climb buildings, sweep chimneys, remove asbestos, or pressure-wash facades — the entire job is hands-on physical work in unstructured real-world environments. Some paperwork like inspection reports could be AI-assisted, but this barely affects overall workload.

children.title: Stonemasons, stone cutters, splitters and carvers
children.rationale: AI cannot cut, carve, shape, or polish stone — the entire job is skilled physical transformation of hard materials using hand tools and power equipment. A few peripheral tasks like quoting or record-keeping have some AI assistance, but the core craft is firmly manual.

children.title: Glaziers
children.rationale: Glazing work is almost entirely physical: measuring, cutting, and fitting glass into buildings or vehicles on-site. AI cannot handle the hands-on installation, and no meaningful share of core tasks can be automated with current tools.

title: Chief Executives, Senior Officials and Legislators
title_de: Geschäftsführer, Vorstände, leitende Verwaltungsbedienstete und Angehörige gesetzgebender Körperschaften

children.title: Managing directors and chief executives
children.rationale: AI can automate financial analysis, performance reporting, and strategic planning support, but the core of this job — making high-stakes decisions, leading people, and representing the organization in negotiations — resists automation. The irreducibly human demands of accountability and stakeholder trust keep exposure moderate.

children.title: Senior government officials
children.rationale: AI can draft policy briefs, analyse budgets, and summarise legislation, but the core of this job — negotiating with foreign governments, making high-stakes political decisions, and leading public institutions — requires human authority and trust. The job sits at moderate exposure because AI augments analytical work without replacing the judgment and accountability at its centre.

children.title: Senior officials of special-interest organisations
children.rationale: AI can draft policy documents, analyze political landscapes, and prepare briefings, but the core of this job—negotiating on behalf of members, lobbying legislators, and representing organizations in high-stakes forums—depends on human relationships and trust. The irreplaceable interpersonal and political dimensions keep exposure moderate.

children.title: Legislators
children.rationale: AI can help legislators draft bills, analyse policy documents, and summarise constituent concerns, but the core work — negotiating political deals, casting votes, and representing constituents — requires human legitimacy and trust. The job is fundamentally about political power and accountability, not information processing.

children.title: Nursing and midwifery associate professionals
children.rationale: AI can help with documentation, scheduling, and basic patient monitoring alerts, but the core work—hands-on patient care, wound treatment, and clinical assessments—requires physical presence and human judgment. The job stays largely human because most tasks involve direct bodily care that robots cannot yet reliably perform.

children.title: Medical assistants
children.rationale: AI can automate appointment scheduling, insurance billing, and medical record entry, but the core job involves hands-on patient care — taking blood, giving injections, and assisting during examinations — that requires physical presence. The majority of tasks are clinical and interpersonal, keeping AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Dental assistants and therapists
children.rationale: AI cannot clean teeth, remove calculus, administer anaesthesia, or assist during physical dental procedures — the core of this job is hands-on clinical work inside a patient's mouth. The small administrative and educational components offer minor AI assistance, but physical presence dominates the role.

children.title: Pharmaceutical technicians and assistants
children.rationale: AI can automate prescription verification, inventory tracking, and insurance claim processing, but dispensing physical medications, counselling patients face-to-face, and handling emergency situations require human presence. The job is a hybrid of regulated physical tasks and information work, keeping exposure moderate.

children.title: Physiotherapy technicians and assistants
children.rationale: AI cannot perform hands-on massage, manual therapy, or physical rehabilitation exercises with patients — the core work is physical touch and in-person movement guidance. children.title: Medical and pathology laboratory technicians
children.title_de: Medizintechniker im Bereich Labor und Pathologie

The job remains anchored in physical manipulation and regulated clinical validation, keeping AI exposure moderate.

children.title: Dispensing opticians
children.rationale: AI can help interpret prescriptions and manage client records, but the core work—physically fitting frames, adjusting eyeglasses, cutting and verifying lenses—requires hands-on manipulation that robots cannot yet reliably perform. children.title: Medical and dental prosthetic technicians
children.rationale: AI can assist with design software and digital scanning for prosthetics and dental devices, but the core work—physically fabricating, fitting, and adjusting custom devices on real patients—requires hands-on skill that robots cannot yet reliably replicate. children.rationale: AI cannot perform the core work of paramedics: physically reaching patients, administering injections, intubating airways, defibrillating, or carrying people to ambulances. children.title: Veterinary technicians and assistants

children.title_de: Fachkräfte in traditioneller und komplementärer Medizin

children.title: Community health workers
children.rationale: AI can help draft health education materials, maintain records, and support data collection, but the core work—visiting families at home, building trust in underserved communities, and navigating crises in person—requires physical presence and human relationship. children.title_de: Fachkräfte im Bereich medizinische Dokumentation und Information

children.title: Health associate professionals not elsewhere classified

children.title: Midwifery associate professionals
children.rationale: AI cannot deliver babies, physically examine newborns, or manage emergency complications during childbirth. The core work is hands-on physical care and real-time clinical judgment, leaving very little for AI to replace.

children.title: Graphic and multimedia designers
children.rationale: AI can now generate images, animations, and design layouts at near-professional quality, automating much of the routine asset creation that fills a graphic designer's workday. children.title: Civil engineers
children.rationale: AI can automate structural calculations, generate design variants, and draft technical reports, but civil engineers spend much of their time on site coordination, client negotiation, and solving unexpected physical problems. The mix of digital analysis and irreducible on-site judgment places this occupation in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Mechanical engineers
children.rationale: AI can accelerate design iteration, simulate stress tests, and generate technical drawings, but core mechanical engineering work requires physical validation, safety certification, and novel problem-solving in complex systems. The job stays moderate because human judgment and regulatory accountability anchor the most critical outputs.

children.title: Industrial and production engineers
children.rationale: AI can automate data analysis, scheduling, and process optimization modelling, but core work requires physical plant inspections, hands-on troubleshooting, and coordinating people and machinery on the factory floor. The mix of digital analysis and irreplaceable physical presence keeps exposure moderate.

children.title: Biologists, botanists, zoologists and related professionals
children.title_de: Biologen, Botaniker, Zoologen und verwandte Berufe

children.title: Mathematicians, actuaries and statisticians
children.rationale: AI can now automate statistical analysis, build predictive models, process large datasets, and generate actuarial projections with high accuracy. However, novel mathematical research, regulatory sign-off on risk models, and client-facing advisory judgment keep humans central.

children.title: Electrical engineers
children.rationale: AI can automate design drafting, simulation, and documentation tasks that electrical engineers do daily. However, the core work—approving physical systems, supervising construction, and ensuring safety compliance—requires on-site judgment that AI cannot yet replace.

children.title: Product and garment designers
children.rationale: AI can generate design sketches, create 3D models, and automate trend research, covering a large share of the digital design workflow. However, physical prototyping, client negotiation, material testing, and manufacturing supervision keep meaningful human work in place.

children.title: Engineering professionals not elsewhere classified
children.rationale: AI can accelerate design simulation, literature research, cost analysis, and report writing for many engineering tasks in this group. However, the core work spans highly physical, safety-critical, and novel domains—nuclear systems, optical prototyping, dismantling—where human judgment and on-site expertise remain essential.

children.title: Chemists
children.rationale: AI can accelerate literature review, data analysis, and report writing, but the core work—designing experiments, synthesizing compounds, and operating lab equipment—requires physical presence and expert judgment. The irreducibly hands-on nature of laboratory chemistry keeps exposure moderate.

children.title: Town and traffic planners
children.rationale: AI can automate data analysis, traffic modelling, GIS mapping, and report drafting that form a large part of this work. However, the job's core value lies in stakeholder negotiation, political judgment, and site-specific decision-making that AI cannot yet replace.

children.title: Environmental protection professionals
children.rationale: AI can accelerate data analysis, report writing, environmental impact assessments, and literature synthesis, which are significant parts of this job. However, field sampling, ecological surveys, site inspections, and regulatory advisory roles require physical presence and expert judgment that AI cannot replace.

children.title: Electronics engineers
children.rationale: AI can accelerate circuit design, simulation, and technical report writing, but core electronics engineering requires physical prototyping, hardware validation, and safety-critical judgment that AI cannot yet reliably perform. The mix of hands-on lab work and complex system design keeps exposure moderate.

children.title: Farming, forestry and fisheries advisers
children.rationale: AI can automate data collection, soil and crop analysis, report writing, and market research that form a significant part of this work. However, on-site fieldwork, hands-on advisory relationships with farmers, and real-world environmental judgment keep human experts central.

children.title: Physicists and astronomers
children.rationale: AI can accelerate data analysis, literature review, and simulation work that physicists do daily. However, designing experiments, interpreting novel results, and operating physical instruments still require human expertise, keeping this occupation at moderate exposure.

children.title: Chemical engineers
children.rationale: AI can accelerate literature review, process simulation, cost estimation, and safety documentation for chemical engineers. However, the core work—designing physical plant equipment, running pilot experiments, and overseeing live chemical processes—requires hands-on judgment that AI cannot yet replace.

children.title: Cartographers and surveyors
children.rationale: AI can automate much of the data processing, map compilation, and GIS analysis that cartographers and surveyors do at a desk, but physical fieldwork — operating instruments on-site, conducting land surveys, and validating boundary measurements — still requires human presence. The mix of automatable office work and resistant fieldwork lands this occupation in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Telecommunications engineers
children.rationale: AI can assist with network design, bandwidth analysis, and documentation, but core work involves specifying physical infrastructure, directing installation crews, and solving novel signal or hardware problems on-site. The mix of physical oversight and deep technical judgment keeps exposure moderate.

children.title: Environmental engineers
children.rationale: AI can accelerate environmental data analysis, report writing, and regulatory compliance checks, but core work requires physical site inspections, field sampling, and engineering judgment on novel contamination problems. The mix of digital analysis and irreplaceable fieldwork anchors this at moderate exposure.

children.title: Geologists and geophysicists
children.rationale: AI can automate data processing, map generation, and report drafting, but core geological work requires field data collection, physical sample analysis, and expert interpretation of ambiguous subsurface conditions. The heavy fieldwork and physical measurement components keep exposure moderate despite significant digital analysis tasks.

children.title: Landscape architects
children.rationale: AI can generate design concepts, produce drawings, analyse site data, and draft reports, but landscape architecture requires physical site inspection, client negotiation, regulatory navigation, and construction oversight. The mix of digital design work and irreplaceable on-site judgment places this occupation in the moderate tier.

children.title: Meteorologists
children.rationale: AI can automate much of the data processing, pattern recognition, and routine forecast generation that meteorologists do daily. However, the job also requires physical instrument calibration, novel research, and expert judgment in high-stakes warnings, keeping it at moderate exposure.

children.title: Mining engineers, metallurgists and related professionals
children.rationale: AI can assist with data analysis, report writing, and mine planning software, but core work involves physical site assessment, directing blasts, and managing underground operations that require on-site human judgment. The job's physical complexity and safety-critical decisions keep AI in a supporting role.

children.title: Lawyers
children.rationale: AI can draft contracts, research case law, and summarize legal documents at near-professional quality, automating a large share of a lawyer's document and research work. children.title: Psychologists

The job stays at moderate exposure because the irreplaceable output is a human relationship, not a document.

children.title: Social work and counselling professionals
children.rationale: AI can help draft case reports, suggest referrals, and organize documentation, but the core work — building trust with vulnerable people, crisis intervention, and protective investigations — requires human presence and judgment. The job stays at moderate exposure because most value is delivered through relationships, not information processing.

children.title: Legal professionals not elsewhere classified
children.rationale: AI can draft legal documents, research legislation, and analyse contracts at near-professional quality, automating a large share of document-heavy tasks. However, the role's core value lies in authenticated judgment, client trust, and legally binding acts that require human presence and accountability.

children.title: Journalists

children.title: Musicians, singers and composers

The job's core value is physical presence and expressive performance, which AI cannot replicate in the concert hall.

children.title: Religious professionals

children.title: Librarians and related information professionals

children.title: Authors and related writers

children.title: Visual artists

children.title: Archivists and curators

The mix of automatable information tasks and irreplaceable physical and curatorial work places this occupation in the moderate tier.

children.title: Economists

children.title: Judges

children.title: Actors
children.rationale: AI can generate scripts and synthetic digital performances, but live acting requires a human body, emotional presence, and real-time audience interaction that AI cannot replicate in 2026. The core work product is a physical, embodied performance, keeping exposure low.

children.title: Philosophers, historians and political scientists
children.rationale: AI can now draft literature reviews, summarize historical sources, run statistical analyses, and generate policy briefs at near-professional quality. children.title: Sociologists, anthropologists and related professionals

children.title: Creative and performing artists not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Bildende und darstellende Künstler, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI cannot replicate live physical performance — acrobatics, magic tricks, clowning, or street art creation in public spaces all require a human body and real-time audience interaction. The job's core value is embodied presence and live spectacle, which AI cannot substitute.

children.title: Dancers and choreographers
children.rationale: AI cannot physically dance, perform live before audiences, or choreograph through embodied movement and rehearsal. The core work is irreducibly physical and interpersonal, keeping AI exposure very low.

children.title: Announcers on radio, television and other media

children.title: Finance managers

children.title: Human resource managers

children.title: Business services and administration managers not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Führungskräfte in der betrieblichen Administration und in unternehmensbezogenen Dienstleistungen, anderweitig nicht genannt
children.rationale: AI can automate report writing, budget analysis, scheduling, and compliance monitoring that make up a significant portion of this role. However, the core value lies in stakeholder management, strategic judgment, and leading people — tasks AI cannot yet replace.

children.title: Policy and planning managers

children.title: Research and development managers

children.title: Advertising and public relations managers

children.title: Systems analysts

The remaining human value lies in interpreting ambiguous requirements, architectural decisions, and accountability — placing this firmly in the very-high-exposure tier.

children.title: Systems administrators
children.rationale: AI can automate routine monitoring, log analysis, scripting, and basic troubleshooting that make up a large share of daily sysadmin work. However, hands-on hardware intervention, complex incident response, and organizational judgment keep human administrators essential.

children.title: Web and multimedia developers
children.rationale: AI can now generate code, write web content, create graphics, and automate SEO tasks — covering the majority of what web and multimedia developers produce daily. Human value increasingly concentrates in creative direction, client requirements gathering, and architectural decisions for complex projects.

children.title: Database designers and administrators
children.rationale: AI can now write SQL queries, generate data models, automate migrations, and assist with database schema design at a professional level. However, complex architecture decisions, performance tuning in production environments, and security governance keep human experts central.

children.title: Database and network professionals not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Fachkräfte für Datenbanken und Netzwerke, anderweitig nicht genannt.
children.rationale: AI can automate threat detection, vulnerability scanning, log analysis, and routine security monitoring, but defending against novel attacks and making high-stakes incident response decisions still requires human judgment. The mix of automatable analytical tasks and irreplaceable adversarial reasoning places this occupation firmly in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Computer network professionals
children.rationale: AI can automate network monitoring, traffic analysis, fault diagnosis, and documentation drafting, but designing network architecture and hands-on hardware configuration still require human judgment and physical intervention. The mix of automatable analytical tasks and irreducible physical and architectural work places this occupation in the high-exposure tier.

children.title: Software and applications developers and analysts not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Entwickler und Analytiker von Software und Anwendungen, anderweitig nicht genannt

children.title: Health care assistants

children.title: Child care workers
children.rationale: AI cannot physically help a child dress, eat, or play, and cannot supervise children's safety or manage their behaviour in person. The job is almost entirely hands-on physical care and human presence, leaving very little for AI to replace.

children.title: Teachers’ aides
children.rationale: AI cannot physically supervise children, assist a student with motor skills, or respond to a child's emotional distress in the classroom. The job is fundamentally about in-person care, physical presence, and human relationship-building with children.

children.title: Personal care workers in health services not elsewhere classified
children.title_de: Betreuungsberufe im Gesundheitswesen, anderweitig nicht genannt

children.title_de: Reinigungspersonal und Hilfskräfte in Büros, Hotels und anderen Einrichtungen

children.rationale: AI cannot vacuum floors, scrub toilets, iron clothes, or wash dishes — all core tasks here require physical presence and manual dexterity in unstructured home environments. This job is almost entirely physical labour with no meaningful digital work product, placing it at minimal AI exposure.

children.title: Hand launderers and pressers
children.rationale: AI cannot wash, iron, or press garments by hand — these are physical tasks requiring dexterous manipulation of delicate fabrics in unstructured environments. Robotics capable of this work at commercial scale does not yet exist, keeping AI exposure very low.

children.rationale: AI cannot wash, vacuum, polish, or detail a vehicle — these are hands-on physical tasks requiring dexterity in unstructured environments. Automated car washes exist but handle only basic exterior washing, leaving the skilled manual work of detailing firmly human.

children.title: Other cleaning workers
children.rationale: AI cannot physically scrub carpets, pressure-wash walls, unblock sewers, or maintain pool water chemistry — the entire job is hands-on work in varied physical environments. A small administrative fringe (scheduling, customer communication) has some AI assistance, but the core work is firmly manual.

children.title: Sports coaches, instructors and officials
children.rationale: AI can assist with performance analytics, training plan generation, and officiating support, but the core work—physically demonstrating techniques, motivating athletes in real time, and making split-second officiating calls—requires embodied presence. The job is anchored in physical transformation of food and hands-on supervision, keeping exposure very low.

children.title: Interior designers and decorators

The mix of digital design tasks and hands-on physical execution places this occupation in the high-exposure tier, not very high.

children.title: Fitness and recreation instructors and program leaders
children.title_de: Trainer und Betreuer im Bereich Fitness und Erholung
children.rationale: AI cannot demonstrate a squat, spot a client lifting weights, lead a group hike, or physically guide a horse rider — the core work is embodied, real-time, and safety-critical. The job is anchored in physical presence and human motivation, keeping AI exposure low.

children.title: Photographers
children.rationale: AI can now generate photorealistic images, automate photo editing, and handle retouching tasks that once required skilled post-processing work. However, operating cameras on location, directing subjects, and building client relationships still require a human presence, keeping the score moderate.

children.title: Religious associate professionals
children.rationale: AI cannot perform religious ceremonies, provide genuine spiritual counselling, or embody a monastic vow — the core of this work is human presence, faith, and relational trust. The job is deeply interpersonal and physically enacted, leaving almost no room for AI substitution.

children.title: Other artistic and cultural associate professionals
children.title_de: Sonstige Fachkräfte in Gestaltung und Kultur
children.rationale: Most work in this group involves physical setup, operation, and coordination on live sets and stages — rigging lights, operating pyrotechnics, managing props, performing stunts — tasks AI cannot physically execute. children.title: Athletes and sports players

children.title: Social welfare managers
children.title_de: Führungskräfte in der Erbringung von Dienstleistungen der sozialen Institutionen

children.title: Child care services managers
children.title_de: Führungskräfte in der Erbringung von Dienstleistungen der Kinderbetreuung

children.title_de: Führungskräfte in der Produktion in Land- und Forstwirtschaft

The core work is physical operation of a vehicle in unpredictable real-world environments, which AI cannot yet reliably perform autonomously.

children.title: Bus and tram drivers
children.rationale: AI can assist with route optimization and ticketing, but driving a bus or tram through urban traffic, managing passengers, and responding to emergencies requires physical presence and real-time judgment. Autonomous vehicle technology exists but is not yet deployed at scale for public transit in complex urban environments.

children.title: Earthmoving and related plant operators

Regulatory mandates, safety liability, and the physical complexity of rail operations keep automation limited for now.

children.title: Crane, hoist and related plant operators

children.rationale: AI cannot physically operate a crane, rig loads, or react to dynamic site conditions in real time — these require hands-on control, spatial judgment, and split-second physical responses. children.title: Railway brake, signal and switch operators

children.rationale: AI can assist with scheduling, monitoring, and signal optimization, but the core work—physically coupling wagons, operating switches, inspecting brakes, and responding to live track incidents—requires hands-on presence in unpredictable environments. children.title: Lifting truck operators

children.title: Motorcycle drivers

children.title: Mobile farm and forestry plant operators

children.title: Metal working machine tool setters and operators

children.title: Toolmakers and related workers

Some peripheral tasks like reading plans or recording production data have AI tools, but the core work is firmly physical.

children.title: Bicycle and related repairers
children.rationale: AI cannot tighten brake cables, true wheels, or replace worn drivetrain components — the core work is hands-on mechanical repair of physical objects. children.title: Metal polishers, wheel grinders and tool sharpeners

children.title: Statistical, finance and insurance clerks

children.title: Tree and shrub crop growers
children.rationale: AI can help with crop monitoring via sensors and market analysis, but the core work—pruning trees, harvesting fruit, managing soil, and operating machinery in variable outdoor conditions—is physical and resists automation.
